Theorem 1.4.1. Interchange of Differentiation and Limit of a Sequence of Functions.

Suppose that \(\{f_n(x)\}\) is a sequence of functions differentiable on \([a, b]\) and that \(\{f_n(x_0)\}\) converges for some \(x_0 \in [a, b]\text{.}\) Suppose, in addition, that \(\{f_n'(x)\}\) converges uniformly on \([a, b]\text{.}\) Then \(\{f_n(x)\}\) converges uniformly on \([a, b]\) to a differentiable function \(f(x)\text{,}\) and
\begin{equation*} f'(x)=\lim_{n\to \infty} f_n'(x). \end{equation*}

Proof.

in-context
